source_record.title: The 2026 Andes virus outbreak: What genomic stability reveals about virulence.
2026 Cruise Ship-associated Andes Hantavirus Outbreak and Public Health Response.
source_record.abstract: Andes virus (ANDV) is an exceptional hantavirus for which human-to-human transmission has been repeatedly reported, and it causes hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome with high lethality in South America. This review aims to summarize the epidemiological and clinical features of hantavirus infections and their transmission routes, and to examine transmission patterns within a confined, high-density environment and future public health response strategies, using the 2026 ANDV outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship as a case study. Drawing on publicly available reports from the World Health Organization (WHO), the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control, the Pan American Health Organization, and the Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency, as well as relevant literature, we reviewed the characteristics of hantavirus infections and this outbreak. The MV Hondius departed from Ushuaia, Argentina, on April 1, 2026. On May 2, the UK International Health Regulations (IHR) National Focal Point formally alerted the WHO to a cluster of severe acute respiratory illnesses. By May 27, 13 individuals (11 confirmed and 2 probable cases) had been infected, and 3 had died. Genomic analysis revealed no more than one single-nucleotide polymorphism difference among patients, indicating onboard human-to-human transmission following a single or a very small number of zoonotic spillover events; the effective reproduction number was estimated to be 0.7 on May 22. Major factors contributing to transmission included advanced age (mean, approximately 65 years), shared cabins, communal indoor spaces, and close caregiving or clinical contact. This outbreak demonstrates that pathogens with limited human-to-human transmissibility can nevertheless be amplified in a closed, high-density environment. Prompt identification, international coordination under the IHR, and efficient risk communication were central to controlling the outbreak, and preparedness for potential importation requires routine assessment of travel history, surveillance for suspected cases, and access to rapid diagnostic testing.

source_record.title: Drivers of Host-Pathogen Community Assemblages in European Forests and Urban Green Spaces.
source_record.abstract: Despite advances in understanding infectious diseases, the persistence and re-emergence of wildlife pathogens continue to raise public and veterinary health concerns. This study investigates the relationship between biodiversity and rodent-borne diseases in Europe, focusing on habitat alterations and their impact on rodent diversity. We present host-pathogen data from 21 temperate forest sites and eight urban green spaces throughout five European countries, environments where rodents are abundant and human/domestic animals-wildlife interactions are likely to occur. From 2020 to 2022, 3766 specimens comprising 15 different small mammal species were analyzed. Samples were screened for bacteria via 16S rRNA sequencing or PCR, and for viral antibodies using immunofluorescent assays. Pathogens from several genera, including Bartonella, Borrelia, Mycoplasma, Anaplasma, Neoehrlichia, Leptospira, Orthohantavirus, and Orthopoxvirus, were detected at non-negligible prevalence in 11 host species. Host community composition differed between habitats, with more urban adapters in parks than in forests. Pathogen richness increased with an increase in host species diversity, supporting the "host-diversity begets parasite-diversity" hypothesis, though not with anthropization. The absence of some vector-transmitted parasites in urban areas suggests a shift in pathogen community driven by human impact. Host species and intrinsic factors were dominant explanatory variables for Mycoplasma species and Sarcocystidae, while extrinsic environmental and climatic factors influenced variations in several vector-transmitted pathogens. Apodemus sylvaticus and Clethrionomys glareolus served as important connector hosts in urban spaces and temperate forests, respectively. These results improve our understanding of the complex local host-pathogen system, aiding future management decisions and supporting the public health sector.

source_record.title: Neurological Manifestations of Hantavirus Infection: A Review.
source_record.abstract:
Importance

Hantaviruses have caused several recent human disease outbreaks. They carry a high mortality rate, and some species can spread from person to person although they are typically spread from rodents to humans. Neurological complications have been documented but are poorly understood.

Observations

Broadly, 2 types of syndromes have been associated with hantaviruses. The pulmonary syndrome carries a high mortality rate and can result in pulmonary and cardiac failure. Neurological involvement is typically secondary to hypoxic and ischemic injury or metabolic dysfunction. Another manifestation of the infection that is associated with some species of the virus is termed hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome. Some patients may develop a meningoencephalitis or pituitary apoplexy resulting in sudden visual loss and pituitary dysfunction. Rarely, seizures, myelitis, and peripheral neuropathy may occur. Although there are no specific antiviral drugs approved for treatment of hantavirus, there are several that showed efficacy in preclinical trials. Management is supportive care and treatment of symptoms.

Conclusions and relevance

Neurological manifestations of hantavirus infection are uncommon but can be severe. Prospective studies and experimental models are needed to better characterize these manifestations, understand pathophysiology, identify therapeutic targets, and develop guidelines for management.

source_record.title: Distribution and richness of Orthohantavirus reservoir hosts: Implications for zoonotic risk in Southern South America.
source_record.abstract: Orthohantaviruses represent a major zoonotic threat in Southern South America (SSA), where 13 native rodent species are recognized reservoir hosts of 15 different viral genotypes. The information on their geographic distribution remains scattered, limiting regional assessments of exposure risk. We compiled and curated occurrence records for the Orthohantavirus reservoir hosts in SSA and used ecological niche modeling to estimate their potential distributions. We integrated binary presence predictions to map reservoir and genotype richness as well as reservoir assemblages. The richness maps were combined with gridded human population data to estimate the number of people potentially exposed. Most Orthohantavirus reservoir species in SSA are distributed over eastern and northeastern Argentina, parts of Paraguay, Uruguay, and southern Brazil. Hotspots of reservoir richness were concentrated in the Humid Chaco, while the area of high genotype richness extends across the Chaco-Pampean plain. Exposure to a high diversity of pathogenic genotypes (≥7) was estimated to exceed 13 million people under the most precautionary scenario. This study provides the first integrated, region-wide assessment of Orthohantavirus reservoir distributions and richness in SSA, offering an updated ecological baseline to support surveillance, risk mapping, and prevention strategies.

source_record.title: Hantavirus infections in a changing world: epidemiology, pathogenesis, and one health challenges.
source_record.abstract: Hantavirus infections are zoonotic diseases of global relevance, characterised by significant morbidity and mortality and complex ecological dynamics. Transmitted primarily through rodent reservoirs, hantaviruses cause two major clinical syndromes: haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) and hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome (HCPS). Despite being historically confined to specific geographic regions, recent environmental changes, increased human animal interaction, and global mobility have contributed to evolving epidemiological patterns. This review summarises current knowledge on hantavirus transmission, global epidemiology, pathophysiological mechanisms, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches. Particular attention is given to the emerging global epidemiology of Seoul virus (SEOV), an urban-associated hantavirus linked to brown rats and pet rodents. In addition, it explores emerging challenges related to vaccine development, surveillance, and the integration of One Health strategies in the prevention and control of hantavirus infections. Strengthening multidisciplinary approaches and improving early detection remain critical to mitigating the global impact of these infections.

Strengthening multidisciplinary approaches and improving early detection remain critical to mitigating the global impact of these infections.

source_record.title: Hantavirus on a Cruise Ship: What COVID-19 Taught Us.
source_record.abstract: Delayed recognition of the mechanics of aerosol transmission - the primary mode of spread of SARS-CoV-2 - at the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic led to inadequate early recommendations regarding nonpharmaceutical interventions and poor initial messaging about how to reduce infection risk. We highlight key epidemiological details of Andes virus (ANDV) transmission on the MV Hondius cruise ship and during prior reported outbreaks with confirmed person-to-person spread in Argentina, which, together, suggest aerosol spread may be a key mechanism of transmission of ANDV between source cases and susceptible contacts in permissible environmental settings.

source_record.title: Hantavirus Infection: Review of Clinical Management.
source_record.abstract: Hantavirus infection is a rodent-borne zoonotic disease that can present as Hemorrhagic Fever with Renal Syndrome (HFRS) or Hantavirus Cardiopulmonary Syndrome (HCPS). Both diseases are associated with substantial morbidity and mortality. The rise of new Hantavirus species and recurrent outbreaks worldwide has renewed interest in the epidemiology, pathogenesis, diagnosis, and management of this viral infection. A narrative literature review was conducted using published articles, review papers, epidemiological reports, and clinical studies on Hantavirus up to 2026. Relevant evidence regarding epidemiology, pathogenesis, clinical manifestations, diagnostic approaches, treatment, complications, and prevention was analyzed and synthesized. Hantaviruses primarily infect endothelial cells, leading to increased vascular permeability, thrombocytopenia, and capillary leakage through immune-mediated mechanisms. HFRS affects the kidneys, and HCPS is characterized by severe cardiopulmonary symptoms, although overlap between the two syndromes has increasingly been observed. Diagnosis is based on clinical suspicion supported by epidemiological exposure history, serological testing, and virological confirmation from reverse transcription polymerase chain reaction (RT-PCR). Current management remains largely supportive, including hemodynamic stabilization, respiratory support, and renal replacement therapy when indicated. Ribavirin may provide benefit in selected HFRS cases when given early, but evidence remains inconclusive, especially for HCPS. Favipiravir has demonstrated promising antiviral activity in vitro and in animal studies, yet clinical data in humans are still lacking. Preventive measures focus primarily on minimizing exposure to infected rodents and contaminated environments, while vaccine development remains ongoing. Hantavirus infection continues to represent an important global public health concern due to its potential for severe disease and high mortality. Improved understanding of disease pathogenesis, advances in diagnostic methods, and ongoing research into antiviral therapies and vaccines are essential to improve patient outcomes and strengthen future prevention strategies.

output.translated_title_zh: 汉坦病毒感染在变化的世界中的流行病学、发病机制及一体化健康挑战
output.translated_abstract_zh: 汉坦病毒感染是一种具有全球重要性的动物源性疾病，表现为显著的发病率和死亡率，并具有复杂的生态动态。汉坦病毒主要通过啮齿动物宿主传播，导致两种主要临床综合征：出血热伴肾综合征（HFRS）和汉坦病毒心肺综合征（HCPS）。尽管历史上汉坦病毒感染局限于特定地理区域，但近期环境变化、人类与动物接触增加及全球流动性增强，促使流行病学模式发生演变。本文综述了汉坦病毒的传播、全球流行病学、病理生理机制、临床表现及治疗方法的最新知识。特别关注了与棕鼠和宠物啮齿动物相关的城市汉坦病毒——首尔病毒（SEOV）的新兴全球流行病学。此外，探讨了疫苗开发、监测以及在汉坦病毒感染预防和控制中整合一体化健康策略所面临的新挑战。加强多学科方法和改进早期检测对于减轻这些感染的全球影响至关重要。
output.display_summary_zh: 本文综述了汉坦病毒感染的传播途径、全球流行病学及临床表现，重点关注首尔病毒的城市传播及一体化健康策略在预防控制中的作用，强调多学科合作和早期检测的重要性。
output.display_summary_en: This review summarizes hantavirus transmission, global epidemiology, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches, highlighting the emerging urban spread of Seoul virus and the role of One Health strategies in prevention and control, emphasizing multidisciplinary collaboration and early detection.
output.one_sentence_takeaway: Environmental changes and increased human-animal interactions are driving evolving hantavirus epidemiology, necessitating integrated One Health approaches and improved early detection to mitigate global impact.
key_findings: Hantavirus infections cause significant morbidity and mortality with complex ecological dynamics (A1).
Transmission is primarily through rodent reservoirs causing HFRS and HCPS (A2).
Environmental changes, increased human-animal interaction, and global mobility have altered epidemiological patterns (A3).
Seoul virus (SEOV), associated with urban brown rats and pet rodents, shows emerging global epidemiology (A5).
Challenges exist in vaccine development, surveillance, and integrating One Health strategies for prevention and control (A6).
Multidisciplinary approaches and early detection are critical to mitigating global impact (A7).
output.significance: The review highlights the evolving epidemiology of hantavirus infections driven by environmental and societal changes, emphasizing the need for integrated One Health approaches and improved surveillance to reduce global disease burden.
output.limitations: The abstract does not specify study design, sample sizes, or detailed epidemiological data, limiting assessment of evidence strength and specific quantitative conclusions.
output.evidence_strength: The evidence is based on a narrative review summarizing current knowledge without primary data or meta-analysis, limiting strength to expert synthesis.
uncertainties: Lack of detailed epidemiological data and quantitative metrics in the abstract.
No information on vaccine efficacy or specific surveillance outcomes.
Unclear geographic scope beyond mention of global and urban contexts.

output.translated_title_zh: 正汉坦病毒GN/GC刺突蛋白：感染的分子决定因素及抗病毒免疫干预的靶点
output.translated_abstract_zh: 正汉坦病毒是一类全球分布的啮齿动物传播的RNA病毒，常与人类严重且常致命的疾病相关。南美洲地方性流行的安第斯病毒已证实可发生人传人，凸显其大流行潜力，但目前尚无有效治疗方法，且仅有汉坦病毒和首尔病毒的疫苗。抗病毒干预的关键靶点是病毒表面展示的四聚体GN/GC刺突蛋白。本文综述了这些刺突蛋白的分子和细胞学新见解，重点介绍其在病毒感染周期中的作用，如受体介导的细胞进入、膜融合、病毒组装和细胞释放。我们还审视了治疗性抗体的最新进展，并识别了与其可能抑制机制相关的结构保守免疫热点。最后，讨论了基于GN/GC的疫苗研究和临床试验的进展，强调了关键成就及克服正汉坦病毒公共卫生威胁的剩余挑战。
output.display_summary_zh: 正汉坦病毒是一类全球分布的啮齿动物传播RNA病毒，能引发严重致命疾病。安第斯病毒可人传，显示大流行风险。GN/GC刺突蛋白是抗病毒干预的关键靶点，涉及病毒进入、融合、组装和释放。最新研究聚焦其分子机制及治疗性抗体开发，并推进基于GN/GC的疫苗研究和临床试验。
output.display_summary_en: Orthohantaviruses are globally distributed rodent-borne RNA viruses causing severe and often fatal diseases. Andes virus can transmit person-to-person, indicating pandemic potential. The tetrameric GN/GC spikes on the viral surface are key antiviral targets involved in viral entry, fusion, assembly, and exit. Recent advances include molecular insights, therapeutic antibody development, and progress in GN/GC-based vaccine research and clinical trials.
output.one_sentence_takeaway: The GN/GC spikes of orthohantaviruses are critical molecular targets for understanding infection mechanisms and developing antiviral therapies and vaccines.
key_findings: Orthohantaviruses cause severe and often lethal diseases in humans (A1).
Andes virus can transmit person-to-person, highlighting pandemic potential (A2).
No effective treatments exist; vaccines are only available for Hantaan and Seoul viruses (A2).
The tetrameric GN/GC spikes on the viral surface are key targets for antiviral intervention (A3).
GN/GC spikes play roles in receptor-mediated cell entry, membrane fusion, viral assembly, and cell exit (A4).
Recent advances in therapeutic antibody development identify structurally conserved immune hotspots linked to inhibitory mechanisms (A5).
Progress in GN/GC-based vaccine research and clinical trials shows key achievements and remaining challenges (A6).
output.significance: Understanding the molecular determinants of the GN/GC spikes is crucial for developing effective antiviral therapies and vaccines against orthohantaviruses, which pose a significant public health threat due to their severe disease outcomes and pandemic potential.
output.limitations: The abstract does not specify study limitations; detailed limitations are unavailable due to lack of full text (A1-A6).
output.evidence_strength: The evidence is based on a review article summarizing current molecular and clinical research findings; full text not available for detailed evaluation.
uncertainties: Specific molecular mechanisms and structural details of GN/GC spikes are not detailed in the abstract.

research_highlights.title: 汉坦病毒感染在变化的世界中的流行病学、发病机制及一体化健康挑战
research_highlights.summary: 汉坦病毒感染是一种全球重要的动物源性疾病，主要通过啮齿动物传播，导致出血热伴肾综合征和汉坦病毒心肺综合征。环境变化、人类与动物接触增加及全球流动性增强促使流行病学模式演变。首尔病毒作为城市汉坦病毒显示新兴全球流行趋势。疫苗开发、监测及一体化健康策略面临挑战，多学科合作和早期检测对减轻全球影响至关重要。

research_highlights.title: 正汉坦病毒GN/GC刺突蛋白：感染的分子决定因素及抗病毒免疫干预的靶点
research_highlights.summary: 正汉坦病毒为全球分布的啮齿动物传播RNA病毒，安第斯病毒可人传，具大流行潜力。GN/GC刺突蛋白是病毒表面关键抗病毒靶点，参与病毒进入、融合、组装和释放。最新研究聚焦其分子机制、治疗性抗体开发及基于GN/GC的疫苗研究和临床试验，强调其在抗病毒治疗和疫苗开发中的重要性。

output.translated_title_zh: 船上汉塔病毒：COVID-19 给我们的启示
output.translated_abstract_zh: 在 COVID-19 大流行初期，对气溶胶传播机制（SARS-CoV-2 的主要传播方式）的认识延迟，导致早期关于非药物干预措施的建议不足，以及关于如何降低感染风险的初始信息传递不佳。我们重点介绍了 MV Hondius 邮轮上安第斯病毒（ANDV）传播的关键流行病学细节，以及阿根廷先前报告的已确认人际传播的疫情，这些共同表明，在可接受的环境条件下，气溶胶传播可能是 ANDV 在源病例与易感接触者之间传播的关键机制。
output.display_summary_zh: 本研究旨在探讨安第斯病毒在邮轮及阿根廷疫情中的传播机制。作者对 MV Hondius 邮轮上以及阿根廷先前报告的疫情流行病学细节进行了描述性分析。研究发现，气溶胶传播可能是安第斯病毒在源病例与易感接触者之间传播的关键机制。此发现强调了在未来疫情防控中考虑气溶胶传播的重要性。研究仅基于有限的病例描述，缺乏更广泛的数据支持。
output.display_summary_en: The authors review epidemiological details of Andes virus transmission on the MV Hondius cruise ship and prior outbreaks in Argentina. They suggest aerosol spread may be a key mechanism of transmission between source cases and susceptible contacts in permissible environmental settings. The findings highlight the importance of considering aerosol transmission in future outbreak investigations and control measures.

output.translated_title_zh: 变化世界中的汉坦病毒感染：流行病学、发病机制与一体化健康挑战
output.translated_abstract_zh: 汉坦病毒感染是一种具有全球重要性的动物源性疾病，表现为显著的发病率和死亡率，并具有复杂的生态动态。汉坦病毒主要通过啮齿动物宿主传播，导致两种主要临床综合征：出血热伴肾综合征（HFRS）和汉坦病毒心肺综合征（HCPS）。尽管历史上汉坦病毒感染局限于特定地理区域，近期环境变化、人类与动物接触增加以及全球流动性增强，促使其流行病学模式发生演变。本文综述了当前关于汉坦病毒传播、全球流行病学、病理生理机制、临床表现及治疗方法的知识。特别关注了与棕色大鼠和宠物啮齿动物相关的城市汉坦病毒——首尔病毒（SEOV）的新兴全球流行病学。此外，探讨了疫苗开发、监测以及在汉坦病毒感染预防和控制中整合一体化健康策略所面临的新挑战。加强多学科方法和改进早期检测仍是减轻这些感染全球影响的关键。
output.display_summary_zh: 本综述旨在总结汉坦病毒感染的流行病学、发病机制及其在全球变化背景下的挑战。研究基于现有文献，涵盖汉坦病毒的传播途径、临床表现及治疗策略。发现汉坦病毒主要通过啮齿动物传播，导致两种主要疾病：出血热伴肾综合征和汉坦病毒心肺综合征。环境变化和人类活动增加导致流行病学模式变化，特别是首尔病毒在城市环境中的传播引起关注。文章强调了疫苗研发和监测的重要性，并提出整合一体化健康策略以应对未来挑战。研究指出加强多学科合作和早期诊断是控制汉坦病毒感染的关键，但未提供具体的实验数据或临床试验结果。
output.display_summary_en: This review summarizes current knowledge on hantavirus infections, focusing on their epidemiology, pathogenesis, and emerging challenges in a changing world. It highlights that hantaviruses, primarily transmitted by rodent reservoirs, cause two main clinical syndromes: hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome and hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome. Environmental changes and increased human-animal interactions have altered epidemiological patterns, notably with the urban-associated Seoul virus. The review underscores the importance of vaccine development, surveillance, and One Health strategies, emphasizing multidisciplinary approaches and early detection to mitigate global impacts. However, it does not present new experimental or clinical data.

one_sentence_takeaway.text: 汉坦病毒感染因环境变化和人类活动增加而呈现新的流行病学特征，需通过多学科合作和一体化健康策略加强预防和控制。

充分利用浅层基因组掠取技术研究茄属（茄科）刺状谱系的系统分类学潜力
output.translated_text_zh: 前言 基因组掠取（GS）是一种经济高效的植物系统发育基因组学方法，但其从不同基因组区室，特别是全基因组SNP中恢复信息性数据集的能力，在茄属中尚未充分探讨。方法 我们评估了浅层GS在南美刺状茄属谱系系统发育推断中的应用，回收了质体、线粒体和核基因组数据集，包括编码区和全基因组SNP。采用最大似然和共祖方法，在不同SNP过滤策略下推断系统发育树。结果 GS成功回收了完整的质体基因组、细胞器编码区和大量SNP数据集，但未能稳定组装线粒体基因组或回收低拷贝核基因。基于SNP的分析，尤其是核基因组SNP，产生了稳定且支持度高的系统发育树，且不同推断方法结果大致一致。相比之下，编码区数据集，特别是线粒体基因组，表现出较大拓扑不一致，揭示了细胞核与细胞器基因组间的冲突。讨论 结果表明，浅层GS是生成信息丰富SNP数据集以推断茄属系统发育的有效策略，尽管在回收完整线粒体基因组和低拷贝核基因位点方面存在局限。基于SNP的分析显著扩展了GS的系统发育潜力，提供了系统学研究的实用且经济的替代方案。
output.display_summary_zh: 本研究评估了浅层基因组掠取技术在南美刺状茄属系统发育中的应用，成功回收了质体基因组和大量SNP数据，但未能稳定组装线粒体基因组和低拷贝核基因。基于核基因组SNP的系统发育分析结果稳定且支持度高，显示不同推断方法间一致性好。线粒体编码区数据表现出较大拓扑冲突，揭示了细胞核与细胞器基因组间的冲突。研究表明浅层GS是茄属系统发育研究中生成SNP数据的有效且经济的方法。
output.display_summary_en: This study evaluated shallow genome skimming for phylogenetic inference in South American prickly Solanum lineages. It successfully recovered plastid genomes and large SNP datasets but failed to consistently assemble mitochondrial genomes or low-copy nuclear genes. SNP-based analyses, especially from nuclear genomes, produced stable and well-supported phylogenies. Mitochondrial coding regions showed greater topological discordance, indicating cytonuclear conflict. The results demonstrate shallow genome skimming as an effective and cost-efficient strategy for Solanum systematics.

output.translated_title_zh: 气候缓解与适应组合的气候-环境质量筛选框架：综述与行动路线图
output.translated_text_zh: 气候缓解和适应通常通过碳指标进行评估，但气候行动也会在空气、水、土壤、生态系统、公共健康和社区之间重新分配影响。本文综述聚焦于气候-环境质量（CEQ）筛选框架，用于早期气候组合设计。CEQ被提出为一种启发式决策支持协议，而非生命周期评估、环境影响评估、综合评估建模、法律咨询或经过验证的定量指数的替代。我们综合了近期关于技术脱碳、基于自然和土地管理的解决方案、治理与金融以及社区实施的证据，同时区分了高置信度共识声明与依赖情境的证据及示例。该框架通过结合气候效益置信度、环境质量协同效益或权衡风险、公平性和接受度敏感性以及保障/监测报告验证（MRV）准备度，将干预措施分类为无悔、条件性、机会性或避免/降优。我们增加了明确的校准指导、不确定性规则及一个农场-流域示例，展示CEQ如何将广泛的气候声明转化为透明的筛选判断。综合结果支持对清洁能源、能效和甲烷减排等选项的高置信度，前提是保障可信；而生物能源与碳捕集封存（BECCS）、土壤碳信用、硝化抑制剂、湿地恢复、碳捕集与封存/利用（CCS/CCUS）及碳市场则视为依赖情境的选项，需保守核算和本地校准。因此，CEQ最佳用作透明的筛选和学习工具，帮助决策者判断气候组合不仅低碳，而且保护环境质量并具社会持久性。
output.display_summary_zh: 本文提出了一个气候-环境质量（CEQ）筛选框架，用于早期气候缓解与适应组合的设计。该框架结合气候效益置信度、环境质量协同效益或权衡风险、公平性和接受度敏感性以及保障准备度，将干预措施分类为无悔、条件性、机会性或避免/降优。研究综合了技术脱碳、自然基和土地管理解决方案、治理与金融及社区实施的最新证据，明确了不同气候行动的适用性和不确定性。CEQ被定位为启发式决策支持工具，强调透明筛选和本地校准的重要性。该框架支持对清洁能源、能效和甲烷减排的高置信度，同时对BECCS、土壤碳信用等需谨慎对待。
output.display_summary_en: This review presents a Climate-Environmental Quality (CEQ) screening framework for early-stage climate portfolio design. The framework classifies interventions based on climate-benefit confidence, environmental co-benefits or trade-offs, equity, acceptance sensitivity, and safeguards readiness. It synthesizes recent evidence on decarbonization technologies, nature-based solutions, governance, finance, and community implementation. CEQ serves as a transparent decision-support tool to help ensure climate portfolios are low-carbon, environmentally protective, and socially durable.

output.translated_title_zh: LeafRank：一种用于从单细胞系统发育树推断相对适应度的系统发育动力学框架，适用于染色体不稳定肿瘤
output.translated_text_zh: 肿瘤中存在具有多样化生长潜能的癌细胞，这些潜能塑造了进化轨迹，但在全基因组复制（WGD）和染色体不稳定的情况下，这种适应度多样性仍难以量化。我们提出了 LeafRank，一种利用单细胞 DNA‑测序系统发育树推断个体细胞相对适应度的数学框架。通过多型分支过程模型，LeafRank 整合完整的树拓扑结构，包括分支长度和分叉模式，以估计在由稀有驱动事件驱动的突发进化模式下的边际适应度概率。为考虑 WGD 后异常率升高，我们引入了一种树重标策略，以校正谱系特异性的基因组不稳定性。与聚焦于预定义亚克隆的方法不同，LeafRank 对所有采样细胞进行排名，从而实现对生长异质性的灵活评估。仿真显示，在空间和非空间虚拟肿瘤中均具有高精度。将其应用于卵巢癌，LeafRank 揭示了 WGD 肿瘤中的方向性和并行选择，并识别出在高适应度谱系中富集的反复复制数事件。WGD 谱系并未表现出即时生长优势，而是通过随后的改变获得适应度。
output.display_summary_zh: LeafRank 是一种基于单细胞 DNA‑测序系统发育树的数学框架，用于推断癌细胞的相对适应度。它利用多型分支过程模型整合完整树拓扑，估计在稀有驱动事件驱动的突发进化模式下的适应度概率，并通过树重标策略校正 WGD 后的基因组不稳定性。该方法对所有采样细胞进行排名，能够灵活评估生长异质性。仿真验证显示其在空间和非空间虚拟肿瘤中具有高精度。\n在卵巢癌样本中，LeafRank 发现 WGD 肿瘤存在方向性和并行选择，并识别出高适应度谱系中富集的复制数事件。
output.display_summary_en: LeafRank is a mathematical framework that uses single-cell DNA‑seq phylogenies to infer the relative fitness of cancer cells. It employs a multi-type branching process model to integrate full tree topology, estimating fitness probabilities under punctuated evolutionary regimes driven by rare driver events, and introduces a tree‑rescaling strategy to adjust for lineage‑specific genomic instability following WGD. Unlike methods that focus on predefined subclones, LeafRank ranks all sampled cells, enabling flexible assessment of growth heterogeneity. Simulations demonstrate high accuracy across spatial and non‑spatial virtual tumors. Applied to ovarian cancer, LeafRank reveals directional and parallel selection in WGD tumors and identifies recurrent copy‑number events enriched in high‑fitness lineages.

lead_candidates.reason: 汉坦病毒邮轮疫情官方宣布结束，涉及多起感染和死亡病例，具有较大公共卫生影响

daily_overview.text: 当前汉坦病毒相关报道主要聚焦于一场涉及邮轮的汉坦病毒疫情，该疫情已被世卫组织宣布结束，累计13人感染，3人死亡。相关机构持续监测疫情并发布预防指导。研究方面，汉坦病毒感染的神经系统影响被发现，尽管不常见但可能严重，需进一步研究。综述指出环境变化和人类活动增加导致汉坦病毒流行病学模式变化，特别是城市环境中首尔病毒的传播趋势。沙特阿拉伯等国家呼吁旅客遵守健康准则，关注包括汉坦病毒在内的多种传染病。当前证据不足以判断汉坦病毒疫情的全球趋势，但防控和监测仍是重点。

major_events.summary: 世卫组织宣布与邮轮相关的汉坦病毒疫情结束，累计13人感染，3人死亡，最后一名接触者完成隔离且病毒检测阴性。
沙特阿拉伯当局呼吁旅客在夏季遵守健康准则，持续监测包括汉坦病毒、埃博拉、黄热病和季节性流感在内的全球流行病学形势。

research_highlights.summary: 汉坦病毒感染的神经系统表现虽不常见但可能严重，需开展前瞻性研究和实验模型以深入理解其机制并制定管理指南。
综述指出汉坦病毒感染因环境变化和人类活动增加呈现新的流行病学特征，特别是城市环境中首尔病毒的传播趋势，强调疫苗研发、监测和一体化健康策略的重要性。
研究表明气溶胶传播可能是安第斯病毒在邮轮和阿根廷疫情中的关键传播机制，提示未来疫情防控需重视气溶胶传播途径。

geographic_signals.summary: 汉坦病毒邮轮疫情涉及多个国家，病毒传播和消毒措施在荷兰等地进行，阿根廷报告新增病例并向多国提供检测试剂。
沙特阿拉伯关注全球多国汉坦病毒疫情，持续与国际卫生组织协调监测。

pathogen_signals.summary: 汉坦病毒持续引发关注，涉及多种临床表现，包括出血热伴肾综合征和汉坦病毒心肺综合征，且神经系统症状被发现。
安第斯病毒（ANDV）在邮轮和阿根廷疫情中可能通过气溶胶传播，提示病毒传播机制多样化。

host_signals.summary: 汉坦病毒主要通过啮齿动物宿主传播，特别是棕色大鼠和宠物啮齿动物，首尔病毒在城市环境中呈现新兴流行趋势。

watchlist.summary: 汉坦病毒神经系统影响的研究尚处于初步阶段，缺乏具体病例数据和官方确认，需关注未来相关研究进展。
沙特阿拉伯提及多国汉坦病毒疫情但无官方确认，需关注全球疫情监测和官方通报。

部分研究和报道仅基于标题或摘要，缺乏详细数据和权威声明。

output.translated_title_zh: 观看船只，忽视森林：为什么生态溢出、储存宿主生态学和环境变化仍是北美真正的汉坦病毒威胁。
output.translated_abstract_zh: 汉坦病毒在北美的出现是由储存宿主生态学、环境变异、景观条件和人类暴露之间的相互作用驱动的，持续的环境变化增加了溢出机会，改变了风险地理，并挑战了传统公共卫生监测系统。
output.display_summary_zh: 本研究探讨了生态溢出、储存宿主生态学和环境变化如何成为北美汉坦病毒的主要威胁。作者指出，汉坦病毒的出现受储存宿主生态学、环境变异、景观条件和人类暴露的相互作用影响。持续的环境变化增加了病毒溢出的机会，改变了风险地理分布，并对传统公共卫生监测系统构成挑战。该综述强调了生态因素在病毒传播中的核心作用，并提示需要改进监测方法以应对不断变化的风险。
output.display_summary_en: This review examines how ecological spillover, reservoir host ecology, and environmental change drive hantavirus emergence in North America. It highlights that ongoing environmental change increases spillover opportunities, alters risk geography, and challenges traditional public health surveillance systems.

one_sentence_takeaway.text: 生态溢出和环境变化是北美汉坦病毒出现的主要驱动因素。

research_question.text: 为什么生态溢出、储存宿主生态学和环境变化仍是北美真正的汉坦病毒威胁？

key_findings.finding: 汉坦病毒在北美的出现受储存宿主生态学、环境变异、景观条件和人类暴露的相互作用驱动。
持续的环境变化增加了病毒溢出的机会。
环境变化改变了风险地理分布。
传统公共卫生监测系统面临挑战。

significance.statement: 本综述强调生态因素在汉坦病毒传播中的核心作用，并提示需要改进监测方法以应对不断变化的风险。

output.translated_title_zh: 变化世界中的汉坦病毒感染：流行病学、发病机制和一体化健康挑战。
output.translated_text_zh: 汉坦病毒感染是全球相关的 zoonotic 疾病，具有显著的发病率和死亡率以及复杂的生态动态。汉坦病毒主要通过啮齿动物宿主传播，导致两种主要临床综合征：出血热与肾综合征（HFRS）和汉坦病毒心肺综合征（HCPS）。尽管历史上局限于特定地理区域，最近的环境变化、人与动物的互动增加以及全球流动性导致了流行病学模式的演变。本文综述了关于汉坦病毒传播、全球流行病学、病理生理机制、临床表现和治疗方法的当前知识。特别关注与棕色老鼠和宠物啮齿动物相关的城市汉坦病毒——首尔病毒（SEOV）的新兴全球流行病学。此外，探讨了与疫苗开发、监测以及在预防和控制汉坦病毒感染中整合一体化健康策略相关的新兴挑战。加强多学科方法和改善早期检测仍然是减轻这些感染全球影响的关键。
output.display_summary_zh: 本文综述了汉坦病毒感染的流行病学、发病机制及其在变化世界中的挑战。汉坦病毒主要通过啮齿动物传播，导致出血热与肾综合征和汉坦病毒心肺综合征。文章还探讨了疫苗开发和监测等新兴挑战，以及一体化健康策略在预防和控制汉坦病毒感染中的重要性。
output.display_summary_en: This review summarizes the epidemiology, pathogenesis, and challenges of hantavirus infections in a changing world. Hantaviruses are primarily transmitted through rodent reservoirs and cause significant morbidity and mortality. The article also discusses emerging challenges related to vaccine development and the integration of One Health strategies.

output.translated_title_zh: 对拉沙病毒流行区尼日利亚土地利用对人-啮齿动物界面影响的当地知识
output.translated_text_zh: 拉沙热（LF）在西非是地方性疾病，对农村社区的影响不成比例。LF是由Mammarenavirus lassaense（LASV）引起的，这是一种寄生在Mastomys spp.啮齿动物（多乳鼠）中的阿雷纳病毒。本研究利用当地知识来研究人类定居模式和农业土地利用如何影响对Mastomys spp.种群动态的感知，并影响人-啮齿动物互动，跨越尼日利亚拉沙病毒流行区的多样化社会和生态环境。我们在三个定居模式和景观结构不同的地点使用参与性农村评估（PRA）来（1）总结土地覆盖和农业实践，（2）在不同土地覆盖类型中表征人类土地利用，以及（3）识别与疑似Mastomys spp.的人类互动类型和频率。PRA揭示了在村庄和土地覆盖类型中（1）对Mastomys spp.种群生态学和行为的一致感知模式和（2）报告的人-啮齿动物接触。所有村庄都报告了由于季节性土地清理和稻谷收获而导致Mastomys spp.活动增加的感知。对啮齿动物移动的感知在定居类型之间有所不同，报告称在家庭与农田交错的村庄中，Mastomys spp.在农田和住宅区之间的移动增加。在家庭和田地中与Mastomys spp.的定期接触被广泛报告，尽管接触类型和频率因性别农业职责和日常活动而异，揭示了农业环境中可能被低估的LASV传播途径。PRA数据表明，人类定居模式和农业土地利用调节LASV储备的移动和人-储备界面，可能在村庄内部和整个流行区域内创造出拉沙热暴露风险的变化。
output.display_summary_zh: 拉沙热在尼日利亚农村地区流行，由多乳鼠传播。研究利用当地知识，发现人类定居模式和农业土地利用影响多乳鼠种群动态和人-啮齿动物互动。季节性土地清理和稻谷收获增加了多乳鼠活动。在家庭与农田交错的村庄中，多乳鼠在农田和住宅区之间的移动增加。性别农业职责影响了与多乳鼠的接触类型和频率，揭示了农业环境中的潜在传播途径。
output.display_summary_en: Lassa fever is endemic in rural Nigeria, transmitted by Mastomys spp. rodents. The study uses local knowledge to show that human settlement patterns and agricultural land use affect Mastomys spp. population dynamics and human-rodent interactions. Seasonal land clearing and rice harvest increase Mastomys spp. activity. In villages with homes interspersed with farmland, Mastomys spp. movement between fields and residential areas increases. Gendered agricultural duties influence contact frequency with Mastomys spp., revealing potential transmission pathways in agricultural settings.

output.translated_title_zh: 鼠类肝炎E病毒（Rocahepevirus ratti）：全球证据、知识空白和人畜共患病风险
output.translated_text_zh: 鼠类肝炎E病毒（rHEV）是一种新兴的人畜共患病病原体，全球范围内越来越多地在人类、动物和环境样本中检测到。尽管rHEV属于与人类肝炎E病毒（HEV；Paslahepevirus balayani）同一亚科，后者在非洲是地方性疾病，但在非洲大陆的rHEV生态学方面的了解仅限于与刚果民主共和国和加蓬相关的一例旅行相关病例。作为一种啮齿动物传播的病毒，rHEV由于同步生物储备Rattus norvegicus的广泛存在而构成公共卫生风险。关于肝炎原因不明的有限数据进一步强调了需要积极监测以了解rHEV在人类健康中的分布、流行病学和影响。本综述总结了我们目前对rHEV在人类、动物和环境中的全球分布的了解，强调了通过“一体化健康”方法进行持续监测的重要性。在像尼日利亚这样的国家进行病毒监测，该国有大量猪群和已记录的肝炎E病毒（HEV）循环，可能会改善免疫功能受损和易感人群的医疗保健。此外，病毒进化可能促进宿主适应和传播，从而复杂化控制努力。这些因素强调了需要针对性研究和干预策略，以有效应对rHEV的新兴威胁。
output.display_summary_zh: 鼠类肝炎E病毒（rHEV）是一种新兴的人畜共患病病原体，全球范围内在人类、动物和环境样本中被检测到。rHEV与人类肝炎E病毒（HEV）属于同一亚科，但在非洲的生态学了解有限。rHEV由于同步生物储备Rattus norvegicus的广泛存在而构成公共卫生风险。有限的肝炎原因不明数据强调了需要积极监测rHEV。本综述强调了通过“一体化健康”方法进行持续监测的重要性。在像尼日利亚这样的国家进行监测可能会改善免疫功能受损和易感人群的医疗保健。病毒进化可能促进宿主适应和传播，复杂化控制努力。
output.display_summary_en: Rat hepatitis E virus (rHEV) is an emerging zoonotic pathogen detected globally in human, animal, and environmental samples. rHEV shares a subfamily with human hepatitis E virus (HEV), endemic in Africa, but knowledge about rHEV ecology in Africa is limited. rHEV poses a public health risk due to the widespread presence of its reservoir, Rattus norvegicus. Limited data on hepatitis of unknown origin highlight the need for active surveillance to understand rHEV's distribution and impact on human health. The review emphasizes ongoing surveillance through the One Health Approach. Monitoring in countries like Nigeria, with significant pig populations and documented HEV circulation, could improve healthcare for at-risk populations. Viral evolution may facilitate host adaptation and transmission, complicating control efforts.

output.translated_title_zh: 紧急情况下的监测
output.translated_text_zh: 公共卫生监测是持续、系统地收集、分析和解释健康相关数据。它起到早期警告系统的作用 detects impending outbreaks 可能成为公共health emergencies， assists in monitoring and evaluating the impact of interventions, helps track progress towards specified goals, and monitors and clarifies the epidemiology of health problems, guiding priority-setting and planning and evaluation of public health policy and strategies. Humanitarian emergencies increase the risk of transmission of infectious diseases and other health conditions such as severe malnutrition.
output.display_summary_zh: 紧急情况监测帮助 tracking infectious disease transmission and severe malnutrition risks, guiding public health policy through systematic data analysis and intervention evaluation.
output.display_summary_en: Public health surveillance provides continuous data to act as an early warning system, monitor intervention impacts, and guide policy through systematic data analysis.

output.translated_title_zh: 发现了引起神经症状的咸鼠疫感染
output.translated_text_zh: 关于研究：咸鼠疫感染的神经症状虽然罕见，但可能严重。需要进行前瞻性研究和实验模型，以更好地描述这些症状，理解病理生理学，识别治疗目标，并开发管理指南。
output.display_summary_zh: 咸鼠疫感染的神经症状罕见但严重。需要前瞻性研究和实验模型以更好地理解病理生理学、识别治疗目标和管理指南。
output.display_summary_en: Hantavirus infection's neurological effects are rare but severe. Prospective studies and experimental models are needed to better understand the pathophysiology, identify therapeutic targets, and develop management guidelines.

output.translated_title_zh: 美国公民在非洲检测出埃博拉病毒
output.translated_text_zh: 当局有计划在肯尼亚建立一个针对埃博拉暴发期间被感染的美国人的隔离中心，但在肯尼亚法院下令停止该中心建设后取消了该计划。美国有多种隔离和隔离设施用于传染病，去年有两种被用于从船上运送的病例，该船上发生了咸鼠疫爆发。“我们不排除将需要更严格管理的病例转移到美国，”他说时。
output.display_summary_zh: 美国公民在非洲检测出埃博拉病毒。计划在肯尼亚建立隔离中心，但由于法院命令而取消。美国有多种隔离设施，去年用于从船上运送的病例，该船上发生了咸鼠疫爆发。
output.display_summary_en: A US citizen tested positive for Ebola in Africa. Plans to build a quarantine center in Kenya were scrapped after a court order. The US has multiple isolation facilities, which were used for cases from a cruise ship with a hantavirus outbreak.

output.translated_title_zh: 新的科学目录揭示了对人类最具威胁的病毒
output.translated_text_zh: 新的科学目录揭示了可能对全球健康构成威胁的疾病原因列表，研究人员已经确定了数百种能够感染人类的病毒，并评估了它们的危险性。研究人员在此目录中收集了所有已知的能够感染人类的RNA病毒，以帮助专家和卫生机构了解哪些病毒最容易传播和引起广泛的健康危机。研究人员解释说，绝大多数新出现的能够感染人类的病毒最初都是从动物传播过来的，但是病毒在人类体内的存在并不一定意味着它能够引起大流行，因为病毒需要克服一系列生物学障碍才能在人类之间持续传播。
output.display_summary_zh: 新的科学目录揭示了可能对全球健康构成威胁的疾病原因列表。研究人员已经确定了数百种能够感染人类的病毒，并评估了它们的危险性。目录中收集了所有已知的能够感染人类的RNA病毒，以帮助专家和卫生机构了解哪些病毒最容易传播和引起广泛的健康危机。然而，研究人员指出，病毒在人类体内的存在并不一定意味着它能够引起大流行。病毒需要克服一系列生物学障碍才能在人类之间持续传播。因此，需要密切监测和研究这些病毒，以预防潜在的健康危机。
output.display_summary_en: A new scientific directory reveals a list of disease causes that may pose a global health threat. Researchers have identified hundreds of viruses that can infect humans and assessed their danger. The directory collects all known RNA viruses that can infect humans to help experts and health agencies understand which viruses are most likely to spread and cause widespread health crises. However, researchers note that the presence of a virus in humans does not necessarily mean it can cause a pandemic, as viruses need to overcome biological obstacles to continuously transmit between humans.

output.translated_title_zh: 郵輪上的漢他病毒：COVID-19 教給我們的教訓
output.translated_text_zh: COVID-19 大流行初期，對於SARS-CoV-2 主要傳播方式——空氣傳播機制的延遲認識，導致了早期非藥物干預措施的不充分建議和初始信息傳遞不佳，關於如何降低感染風險。 我們強調了安第斯病毒（ANDV）在MV Hondius郵輪上和阿根廷以前報告的爆發中，具有確認的人際間傳播的流行病學詳細信息，這些信息共同表明，在可允許的環境設置中，空氣傳播可能是ANDV 之間的源病例和易感個體之間傳播的關鍵機制。
output.display_summary_zh: 郵輪上的漢他病毒爆發與COVID-19大流行有相似的空氣傳播特徵。安第斯病毒（ANDV）在郵輪上的傳播和阿根廷以前報告的爆發中，顯示空氣傳播可能是ANDV傳播的關鍵機制。早期對空氣傳播機制的認識不充分，導致了早期非藥物干預措施的不充分建議。這些信息對於未來的公共衛生應對策略具有重要意義。漢他病毒的傳播方式與SARS-CoV-2有相似之處，了解這些相似之處可以幫助我們更好地應對未來的公共衛生挑戰。

output.translated_title_zh: 首次在马来西亚的野生 Rattus 物种中检测和遗传特征分析猪圆环病毒 2
output.translated_text_zh: 猪圆环病毒 2 (PCV2) 是一种主要的猪病原体，最近的研究表明圆环病毒具有更广泛的跨物种传播潜力。在马来西亚雪兰州的病原体监测计划中，对 28 只老鼠和 18 只树鼩进行了筛查。所有样本均检测为汉坦病毒阴性，但分子筛查意外在一只野生 Rattus norvegicus 中检测到 PCV2。系统发育和序列分析证实该病毒属于主要的 PCV2d 基因型，命名为株 Siew/Rat/Feces/2026。该研究报告了首次在马来西亚的 Rattus 物种中检测到 PCV2。这表明野生老鼠可能在马来西亚 PCV2d 的环境循环中发挥作用，强调了持续监测其生态分布和人畜共患潜力的重要性。
output.display_summary_zh: 本研究在马来西亚的野生 Rattus 物种中首次检测到猪圆环病毒 2 (PCV2)，并对其遗传特征进行分析。结果显示该病毒属于 PCV2d 基因型，提示野生老鼠可能在其环境循环中发挥作用，需持续监测其生态分布和潜在的人畜共患风险。
output.display_summary_en: This study reports the first detection of Porcine Circovirus 2 (PCV2) in a wild Rattus species in Malaysia and characterises its genetics. The virus belongs to the PCV2d genotype, suggesting that wild rats may contribute to its environmental circulation, highlighting the need for ongoing surveillance of its ecological distribution and zoonotic potential.

major_events.summary: 与邮轮相关的汉坦病毒疫情已正式结束。

geographic_signals.summary: 北美地区汉坦病毒的出现与环境变异和景观条件的相互作用密切相关，病毒溢出风险地理分布正在发生变化。
